chunk_id: Singapore Stable Assets - Money Market Fund.md_chunk_1
text:
[PAGE: 1]
| Category | Details | | |--------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------| | Definition | A Money Market Fund (MMF) is a type of unit trust that invests in high-quality, short-term, low-risk debt instruments denominated in SGD including government securities, MAS bills, bank deposits, and investment-grade corporate paper. MMFs aim to preserve capital, maintain liquidity, and provide modest yield. | | | Typical Yield Environment (2025/26) | Singapore MMFs generally offer yields ~3.3%–3.6% p.a. in the current interest environment, providing a compelling alternative to other cash instruments. | | | Purpose & Use Cases | - Park idle cash with daily liquidity - Serve as a buffer before deploying into risk assets - Short-term cash preservation with higher yield potential than bank deposits | | | How MMFs Work | - Investors pool cash into the fund - Fund manager invests in short-term securities - Interest earned is passed back to investors after fees - NAV typically remains stable around S$1.00 due to short maturities | | | Regulatory Safeguards (MAS Rules) | - Weighted Average Maturity (WAM): ≤ 60 days - Weighted Average Life (WAL): ≤ 120 days - Liquidity Buffers: ≥10% in daily maturing assets; ≥30% in weekly maturing assets These rules ensure liquidity and limit volatility. | | | NAV & Distribution | Returns may be paid via small NAV increases or reinvested fractionally; MMFs aim to keep NAV stable due to short maturity holdings. | | | Breaking the Buck | Rare situation where NAV falls below S$1.00, typically only in extreme market stress; Singapore MMFs tightly regulated so this risk is historically minimal. | | | Comparison to Other Cash Instruments | MMFs vs T-Bills: • T-Bills offer govt-backed capital guarantee but are slightly lower yield and periodic auction process MMFs vs Fixed Deposits: • FDs have lock-ins and lower

metadata.source_file: Singapore Stable Assets - Money Market Fund.md

chunk_id: Singapore Stable Assets - Money Market Fund.md_chunk_2
text:
[PAGE: 1]
minimal. | | | Comparison to Other Cash Instruments | MMFs vs T-Bills: • T-Bills offer govt-backed capital guarantee but are slightly lower yield and periodic auction process MMFs vs Fixed Deposits: • FDs have lock-ins and lower yields (0.8%–1.6% p.a.) vs MMFs’ flexible liquidity and higher yields MMFs vs High-Yield Savings Accounts (HYSAs): • HYSAs may offer high conditional rates but require hoops; MMFs easier to manage and offer consistent market-based return | | | Capital Guarantee & Insurance | - Not capital guaranteed (since MMFs are investment products) - Not SDIC-insured - Funds are held in custody by an independent trustee (assets segregated). | | | Liquidity / Access | - Redemption typically takes T+1 to T+3 business days - Not instant use like ATM but still highly liquid compared to term products | | | Fees & Expense Ratios (Typical) | - Total Expense Ratio (TER) for MMFs generally ~0.15%–0.45% p.a. - TER is deducted from returns daily and affects net yield | | | Tax Treatment (Singapore) | - Capital gains: Not taxable - Dividends/distributions: Not taxable - No withholding tax for local SGD MMFs | | | Minimum Investment | Often as low as SGD 1 via platforms; direct fund house minimums may be higher. | | | Risks | - Interest Rate Risk: Yields vary with market rates - Credit Risk: Exposure to debt issuers (though high quality) - NAV fluctuation: Although minor, NAV may fluctuate slightly | | | Examples of SGD MMFs (2026 Yield Snapshot) | Approximate yields/net of fees: (indicative) • Fullerton SGD Cash Fund ~2.17% p.a. • Phillip SGD Money Market Fund ~1.81% p.a. • LionGlobal SGD Money Market Fund ~2.28% p.a. • LionGlobal Enhanced Liquidity Fund ~2.36% p.a. • United SGD Money Market Fund ~1.79% p.a. • Lion-MariBank SavePlus

metadata.source_file: Singapore Stable Assets - Money Market Fund.md

chunk_id: Singapore Stable Assets - Money Market Fund.md_chunk_3
text:
[PAGE: 1]
Cash Fund ~2.17% p.a. • Phillip SGD Money Market Fund ~1.81% p.a. • LionGlobal SGD Money Market Fund ~2.28% p.a. • LionGlobal Enhanced Liquidity Fund ~2.36% p.a. • United SGD Money Market Fund ~1.79% p.a. • Lion-MariBank SavePlus ~2.57% p.a. | | | Underlying Instrument Composition | - MAS Bills - Singapore Government securities - Short-term bank deposits - Quality corporate money market securities Composition affects yield and risk profile. | | | Channels to Invest in MMFs | 1. Cash Management Accounts (via platforms): • Bundled solutions (e.g., Simple, Simple Plus) with MMFs inside 2. Online brokerages / Fund platforms: • Direct MMF orders through platforms like FSMOne or POEMS 3. Direct Fund House Subscription: • Direct with fund managers (often higher minimums) | | | Platform & Fee Considerations | - Some robo-advisors bundle platform fees into quoted return - Brokerages may not charge platform fees but may have transaction costs - Always evaluate both TER and any platform fee | | | Practical Tips | - Start with as little as SGD 1 on many platforms - Choose redemption timelines that suit cash flow needs - Compare net yields, not gross yields - Use MMFs for emergency funds or short-term cash buckets | | | Overall Role in Portfolio | MMFs can replace idle cash in low-yield bank deposits, offering better yield with liquidity and minimal risk; they complement fixed deposits and T-Bills depending on objectives. | | | | | | | Investment Pathways Using MMFs | | | | Investment Pathway | Use Case | Characteristics | | Cash Management Account (MMF based) | Park everyday emergency funds | Low risk, daily liquidity, moderate yields | | T-Bills | Government-backed short term | Higher safety, scheduled auctions, often lower yield | | Fixed Deposits |

chunk_id: Singapore Stable Assets - Money Market Fund.md_chunk_4
text:
[PAGE: 1]
| Cash Management Account (MMF based) | Park everyday emergency funds | Low risk, daily liquidity, moderate yields | | T-Bills | Government-backed short term | Higher safety, scheduled auctions, often lower yield | | Fixed Deposits | Locked cash for set period | Guaranteed capital & interest, but low yields & no liquidity | | MMF via Brokerage | Active selection of specific MMF | More control, potential for optimizing fund choice | | Robo-Advisor Cash Product | Plug & play access | Simpler execution, automated reinvestment |

metadata.source_file: Singapore Stable Assets - High yeild saving account policies.md

text:
[PAGE: 1]
| | | | | | | | | | | | | Policy Dimension | Policy Description | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Governing authority | Issued and managed by the Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) on behalf of the Singapore Government. | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Core policy objective | Development of a deep and liquid SGD bond market; SGS issuance is not used for budget deficit financing. | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Fiscal framework | Proceeds from SGS are invested and backed by government assets, aligned with Singapore’s balanced-budget and reserves-based fiscal rules. | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Benchmark role | SGS yields form the risk-free benchmark yield curve for pricing corporate bonds, loans, and derivatives. | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Monetary policy use | SGS are used by MAS for liquidity management and money market operations. | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Credit quality | Conservative issuance policy supports Singapore’s AAA sovereign credit rating. | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Instrument types | Treasury Bills (6M, 1Y), SGS Bonds (2–50Y), Inflation-Linked Bonds (ILBs), Singapore Savings Bonds (SSBs). | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Retail-focused instrument | Singapore Savings Bonds provide capital protection, step-up interest, and monthly redemption at par. | | | | | | | | | | | | |

[PAGE: 1]
| | | | | | | | | Retail-focused instrument | Singapore Savings Bonds provide capital protection, step-up interest, and monthly redemption at par. | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Issuance calendar | Issuance schedules are published in advance to ensure transparency and predictability. | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Auction mechanism | Uniform-price auctions; competitive and non-competitive bidding available. | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Issuance sizing | Determined by market liquidity and demand, not government funding needs. | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Re-openings policy | Existing SGS lines may be re-opened to build benchmark-sized issues. | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Investor eligibility | Open to residents, non-residents, institutional investors, banks, and foreign investors. | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Retail access | Retail investors may participate via SSBs and non-competitive auction bids. | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Primary dealer system | MAS appoints Primary Dealers to support auctions and secondary market liquidity. | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Secondary market policy | PDs are required to provide continuous two-way price quotes. | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Interest income taxation (individuals) | Generally tax-exempt for Singapore resident individuals. | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | |

[PAGE: 1]
| | | | | | | | | | | Interest income taxation (individuals) | Generally tax-exempt for Singapore resident individuals. | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Interest income taxation (corporates) | Taxable unless specific exemptions apply. | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Capital gains taxation | No capital gains tax in Singapore. | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Withholding tax | No withholding tax on SGS interest for most investors. | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Risk management principle | Debt issuance is asset-backed and does not represent fiscal stress or deficit borrowing. | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Transparency standards | High transparency through published auction results, calendars, and yield data. | | | | | | | | | | | | | | |

chunk_id: Singapore Stable Assets - Taxation on Dividend.md_chunk_1
text:
[PAGE: 1]
| Dividend Source / Scenario | Recipient Type | Tax Treatment in Singapore | Conditions / Explanatory Notes | |--------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------|---------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | Dividends from Singapore-resident companies | Individual (Resident / Non-resident) | Not taxable | Singapore operates a one-tier corporate tax system. Corporate tax paid by the company is final; dividends distributed to shareholders are exempt from further taxation. | | Dividends from Singapore-resident companies | Corporate shareholder | Not taxable | Applies regardless of residency status of the shareholder. No dividend withholding tax is imposed. | | Foreign-sourced dividends | Individual (Resident) | Generally not taxable | Foreign dividends received by individuals are exempt unless received through a partnership or linked to a trade or business carried on in Singapore. | | Foreign-sourced dividends | Individual (Non-resident) | Not taxable | Singapore does not tax foreign income of non-residents if not sourced from Singapore. | | Foreign-sourced dividends | Corporate taxpayer | Potentially taxable | Taxable unless exempt under specific provisions (e.g., foreign tax paid, headline tax rate condition, and subject-to-tax requirement). | | REIT distributions (Singapore-listed REITs) | Individual (Resident) | Generally not taxable | Most REIT distributions are tax-exempt for individuals under tax transparency rules, unless received through a partnership or business activity. | | REIT distributions | Individual (Non-resident) | Partially taxable | Certain components of REIT distributions may be subject to withholding tax, depending on income classification. | | REIT distributions | Corporate investor | Depends on structure | Tax treatment depends on whether income qualifies under tax transparency or is deemed trading income. | | Dividends from co-operative societies | Individual | Taxable | Treated as personal income and taxed at prevailing individual income tax rates. | | Dividends received through a partnership | Individual / Corporate | Taxable | Income received via partnerships does

metadata.source_file: Singapore Stable Assets - Taxation on Dividend.md

chunk_id: Singapore Stable Assets - Taxation on Dividend.md_chunk_2
text:
[PAGE: 1]
co-operative societies | Individual | Taxable | Treated as personal income and taxed at prevailing individual income tax rates. | | Dividends received through a partnership | Individual / Corporate | Taxable | Income received via partnerships does not automatically qualify for dividend exemption. | | Dividends received as part of a business or trading activity | Individual / Corporate | Taxable | Considered revenue income rather than passive investment income. | | Dividend withholding tax (general dividends) | Any recipient | Not applicable | Singapore does not levy withholding tax on standard dividends. | | Dividend reporting requirement | Individual | Only taxable dividends reported | Exempt dividends do not need to be declared in the annual income tax return. |
